title: Operator TUI scrollbars, focus-aware scrolling and mouse wheel support
purpose: Add consistent scrolling to all important Operator TUI areas, including chat windows, AI/status panels, artifact lists, logs, markdown/mermaid center views and other center VisualViewport views. Scrolling must be focus-aware, keyboard-accessible via PageUp/PageDown and mouse-wheel controllable where terminal mouse support exists.
goal: Every scrollable TUI area owns a ScrollContext. A FocusManager decides which ScrollContext receives PageUp/PageDown, mouse wheel and line-scroll commands. Each visible scrollable area can show a compact scrollbar indicator. Existing focus keybindings must be reused or extended instead of introducing conflicting shortcuts.

context.user_goal: Chatfenster und jeder Bereich sollen eine Scroll-Leiste bekommen, möglichst mit Mausrad-Steuerung und Bild auf/Bild ab. Es soll geprüft werden, ob es bereits Fokus-Wechsel-Tastenkombinationen gibt.

current_keybinding_observation.important_conflict_note: F7 is already used for focus_left in the current keybinding config. Do not reuse F7 for the view-switcher overlay unless the keybinding plan is updated consistently.

context.design_decision: Build one shared scroll subsystem instead of adding independent ad-hoc offsets to each panel. Each panel/view registers a ScrollContext with content height, viewport height, current offset and rendering metadata. Keyboard and mouse events route through FocusManager to the active ScrollContext.
visual_rules: Scrollbar is compact and terminal-friendly, usually one rightmost column inside the panel border or a small textual indicator when space is tight.
Scrollbar must not corrupt ANSI/Sixel/Kitty image output; image/protocol views may expose scroll controls via surrounding viewport chrome rather than inside raster frames.
Focused panel should be visibly marked so PageUp/PageDown target is clear.
Mouse wheel should scroll the panel under the cursor when coordinates are available; otherwise scroll the currently focused panel.
Scrolling must not steal input from chat text editing when the user is editing a multiline input unless explicitly configured.
non_goals: Do not require mouse support for the TUI to be usable.
Do not make scrolling depend on GPU/image protocols.
Do not make every renderer handle scrollbars internally; scrollbars are panel chrome unless a view explicitly owns text scrolling.
Do not introduce shortcut conflicts with existing focus and chat keybindings.

milestones.title: Scrollbar rendering in panels and views

tasks.title: Document focus-aware scrolling architecture
tasks.description: Document shared ScrollContext, ScrollManager, FocusManager and MouseRouter responsibilities.
acceptance_criteria: Docs include a Mermaid flowchart showing keyboard and mouse events routed to the active ScrollContext.
Docs list all initial scrollable areas.
Docs explain how focused panel is detected and visually marked.
Docs explicitly mention existing focus keybindings Ctrl+W, Ctrl+E, F7 and Ctrl+D and how they are reused/extended.

tasks.title: Implement ScrollContext model
tasks.description: Add a reusable model for scrollable content state.
acceptance_criteria: ScrollContext contains id, label, content_height, viewport_height, offset, focusable, visible and optional cursor/selection metadata.
ScrollContext exposes scroll_line_up/down, scroll_page_up/down, scroll_home/end and clamp methods.
Offset is always clamped between 0 and max_scroll.
Page size is derived from viewport_height with a configurable overlap of at least one line.
Unit tests cover empty content, small content, exact-fit content and large content.

tasks.title: Implement ScrollManager registry
tasks.description: Manage all active ScrollContext instances for current TUI layout.
acceptance_criteria: ScrollManager can register, update, remove and retrieve ScrollContext instances by id.
ScrollManager can return visible/focusable contexts in layout order.
ScrollManager removes stale contexts when panels/views disappear.
ScrollManager exposes diagnostics for active context, total contexts and scroll offsets.
Tests verify context lifecycle and stale cleanup.

tasks.title: Implement FocusManager for panels and scroll contexts
tasks.description: Track focused TUI area and active ScrollContext, reusing existing focus actions.
acceptance_criteria: FocusManager supports focus ids for chat_panel, ai_panel, artifact_panel, log_panel, main_content_panel and center_viewport.
FocusManager can cycle focus forward/backward and move left/right where layout order supports it.
Existing actions cycle_focus_or_channel, chat_focus, focus_left and focus_right can delegate to FocusManager.
FocusManager returns the active ScrollContext id for scroll commands.
Focus changes update visible status or panel chrome so the user knows what will scroll.

tasks.title: Add keyboard scroll actions to keybinding config
tasks.description: Register PageUp/PageDown and optional line/home/end scroll shortcuts without conflicting with existing shortcuts.
acceptance_criteria: Actions scroll_page_up and scroll_page_down are registered with keys pageup and pagedown.
Optional actions scroll_line_up/down and scroll_home/end are registered only if they do not conflict with text input navigation.
Keybinding conflict detection reports any duplicate active-area bindings.
Shortcut help/footer can show PgUp/PgDn for active scrollable panel.
F7 remains focus_left unless a separate keybinding cleanup explicitly changes it.

tasks.title: Route PageUp/PageDown to active ScrollContext
tasks.description: Make keyboard scrolling operate on the currently focused panel/view.
acceptance_criteria: PageUp scrolls the active ScrollContext up by one page.
PageDown scrolls the active ScrollContext down by one page.
If no scrollable context is focused, the TUI shows a concise status message instead of failing.
Scrolling invalidates/redraws only the affected panel/view where possible.
Chat input editing remains usable and is not accidentally replaced by scroll behavior unless focus is on chat history panel rather than input buffer.

tasks.title: Extend focus keybindings and footer help for scroll target clarity
tasks.description: Make it visible which area currently receives scroll commands.
acceptance_criteria: Footer/status shows active focus target, for example 'Focus: Chat' or 'Focus: Center'.
Footer/status shows scroll hints when the active focus target is scrollable, for example 'PgUp/PgDn scroll'.
Ctrl+W continues to cycle focus/channel as currently intended or is refined with documented behavior.
Ctrl+E continues to focus chat and should distinguish chat input from chat history where needed.
Focus-left/right actions update FocusManager state and active ScrollContext.

tasks.title: Add chat-control commands for scrolling and focus
tasks.description: Allow Snake Chat command mode to drive scroll and focus for E2E and accessibility.
acceptance_criteria: Commands /focus chat, /focus center, /focus artifacts and /focus logs are supported where corresponding panels exist.
Commands /scroll up, /scroll down, /scroll pageup, /scroll pagedown, /scroll top and /scroll bottom are supported.
Commands route through the existing parser/policy/dispatcher pipeline.
Autonomous E2E mode can allowlist these commands for non-human tests.
Denied or unavailable scroll commands return a structured reason.

tasks.title: Enable terminal mouse event support where supported
tasks.description: Capture mouse wheel events from the terminal/TUI framework without making mouse support mandatory.
acceptance_criteria: Mouse support can be enabled/disabled by config.
When unsupported, the TUI still works with keyboard scrolling.
Mouse wheel up/down events are normalized to scroll_line_up/down or configurable line counts.
Mouse event parsing does not break existing click/selection/snake mouse behavior.
Diagnostics show whether mouse wheel support is active.

tasks.title: Implement MouseRouter hit-testing for panels
tasks.description: Route mouse wheel to the panel under the cursor when coordinates are available.
acceptance_criteria: MouseRouter knows current panel rectangles and associated ScrollContext ids.
Wheel events over a scrollable panel scroll that panel even if it is not focused, unless config says focus-only.
Wheel events over non-scrollable areas fall back to active focused ScrollContext or no-op with status.
Hit-testing handles resized terminal layouts.
Tests cover cursor over chat, artifacts, center viewport and footer/no-scroll area.

tasks.title: Integrate mouse wheel with AI snake and artifact mouse behavior
tasks.description: Avoid conflicts between mouse wheel scrolling, mouse-follow snake behavior and artifact interactions.
acceptance_criteria: Mouse movement/click behavior used by snake/artifacts continues to work.
Mouse wheel events are treated as scroll input, not movement target updates, unless explicitly configured.
Artifact panel can scroll by wheel and still support artifact selection/opening.
Snake mouse-follow mode does not jump or misinterpret wheel-only events as target movements.
Tests or smoke scenarios cover mouse wheel while snake mode is active.

tasks.title: Implement compact terminal scrollbar renderer
tasks.description: Render a simple scrollbar indicator for text panels and scrollable viewport chrome.
acceptance_criteria: Scrollbar renderer can compute thumb position and size from content_height, viewport_height and offset.
Scrollbar uses terminal-friendly characters and has a plain ASCII fallback.
Scrollbar is hidden or shown as full-track when content fits without scrolling according to config.
Scrollbar does not reduce readable content unexpectedly unless the panel reserves a column for it.
Snapshot tests verify scrollbar output for top, middle, bottom and no-scroll states.

tasks.title: Add scrollbars to chat and AI panels
tasks.description: Make chat history and AI/status panels independently scrollable.
acceptance_criteria: Chat history panel has its own ScrollContext independent from chat input buffer cursor.
AI/status panel has its own ScrollContext when content exceeds visible height.
Scrollbar appears when content is scrollable or according to config.
New messages can either auto-stick to bottom when already near bottom or preserve user scroll position when reading older messages.
Tests cover new message append behavior while scrolled up and while at bottom.

tasks.title: Add scrollbars to artifacts, logs and main content panels
tasks.description: Make non-chat panels scroll consistently through the same ScrollManager.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact list has independent scroll offset and scrollbar.
Log/output panel has independent scroll offset and scrollbar.
Main content/detail panel has independent scroll offset and scrollbar.
Selection cursor and scroll offset stay synchronized when moving through artifact/list entries.
Panel-specific scroll states survive redraws and terminal resize where possible.

tasks.title: Integrate scrolling with center VisualViewport text views
tasks.description: Allow scrollable center views such as Markdown/Mermaid and diagnostics to participate in the shared scroll system.
acceptance_criteria: Markdown/Mermaid document view exposes a ScrollContext for long documents.
Renderer diagnostics view exposes a ScrollContext when diagnostics exceed viewport height.
Image/protocol-based center views do not get corrupted by text scrollbars; viewport chrome handles scrollbar where needed.
Mouse wheel over center viewport scrolls the center view when it is scrollable.
PageUp/PageDown scroll center viewport when center has focus.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for ScrollContext and ScrollManager
tasks.description: Verify core scrolling behavior without terminal dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover line/page/home/end scrolling.
Tests cover clamp behavior for small and large content.
Tests cover context registration, update and removal.
Tests cover diagnostics and active context selection.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for FocusManager and keyboard routing
tasks.description: Verify that focus selection determines which panel scrolls.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover Ctrl+W-style focus cycling across registered focus targets.
Tests cover focus_left/focus_right behavior for layout order.
Tests cover PageUp/PageDown routed to active ScrollContext.
Tests cover no active scroll context producing a clean no-op/status result.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for MouseRouter wheel hit-testing
tasks.description: Verify mouse wheel events route to the correct panel based on coordinates.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover wheel over chat panel, artifact panel, center viewport and non-scrollable footer.
Tests cover fallback to focused context when coordinates are missing or outside panels.
Tests cover mouse support disabled path.
Tests cover mouse wheel does not trigger snake movement target updates.

tasks.title: Add E2E/autonomous chat-control scroll scenario
tasks.description: Use autonomous Snake Chat mode to test focus and scroll without a human.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture focuses chat, scrolls page up/down and asserts chat scroll offset changes.
Fixture focuses center viewport, opens or selects markdown view, scrolls page down and asserts center offset changes.
Fixture attempts to scroll non-scrollable area and asserts a clean denied/no-op result.
Test runs without terminal graphics, GPU, Mermaid, browser or mouse support.

tasks.title: Document usage and troubleshooting for scrolling
tasks.description: Make scrolling discoverable and explain terminal limitations.
acceptance_criteria: Docs list keyboard controls: Ctrl+W focus cycle, Ctrl+E chat focus, F7 focus left, Ctrl+D focus right, PgUp/PgDn scroll.
Docs explain mouse wheel support depends on terminal mouse reporting and can be disabled.
Docs explain how focused panel is indicated.
Docs include troubleshooting for mouse wheel not working, wrong panel scrolling and chat input vs chat history focus.

summary_notes: There are already focus-related keybindings: Ctrl+W, Ctrl+E, F7 and Ctrl+D.
F7 is currently occupied by focus_left, so other TODOs should not reuse it for unrelated overlay toggles without a keybinding cleanup.
PageUp/PageDown should scroll the active/focused ScrollContext.
Mouse wheel should scroll the panel under the cursor when coordinates are available, otherwise the focused panel.
Scrollbars should be shared panel chrome, not duplicated inside every renderer.

end_summaries.title: Expected result after completion
items: Every important TUI area can scroll independently.
The active scroll target is clear through focus indication and footer hints.
Mouse wheel scrolls the panel under the cursor where terminal support exists.
Chat history, artifact lists, logs and center Markdown/Mermaid views get compact scrollbars.
Autonomous E2E tests can verify scroll/focus behavior without human interaction.
